title: limnanthemum cristatum
intro: Limnanthemum cristatum, also known as the Crested Marshweed, is a perennial aquatic plant species native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Limnanthemum cristatum include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and can also be consumed as a tea to harness its medicinal properties.
